title: Treatment of Hemorrhoids
license_notes: Use as government source support for hemorrhoid treatment concepts and urgent-care symptoms. Avoid implying NIDDK endorses local discharge wording.
supports: Home treatment may include fiber, fluids, stool softeners when recommended, warm baths, and over-the-counter creams or suppositories.
Severe anal pain with rectal bleeding, especially with abdominal pain, diarrhea, or fever, should prompt medical care right away.
Clinician-directed office procedures or surgery may be needed when symptoms do not improve.
